heading: Alternative motor vehicle credit
label: 30B Alternative motor vehicle credit
notes: References in TextThe Clean Air Act, referred to in text, is , , which is classified generally to chapter 85 (§ 7401 et seq.) of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare. Title II of the Act, known as the National Emissions Standards Act, is classified generally to subchapter II (§ 7521 et seq.) of chapter 85 of Title 42. Sections 202(i), 209(b), and 243(e)(2) of the Act are classified to sections 7521(i), 7543(b), and 7583(e)(2), respectively, of Title 42. (b)(3)(B) and (h)(2), is the date of enactment of , which was approved .Pub. L. 109–58Aug. 8, 2005The date of the enactment of the Energy Tax Incentives Act of 2005, referred to in subsec. (e)(2), is the date of enactment of title XIII of , which was approved .Pub. L. 109–58Aug. 8, 2005

children.text: the new qualified fuel cell motor vehicle credit determined under subsection (b),
the new advanced lean burn technology motor vehicle credit determined under subsection (c),
the new qualified hybrid motor vehicle credit determined under subsection (d),
the new qualified alternative fuel motor vehicle credit determined under subsection (e), and
the plug-in conversion credit determined under subsection (i).

children.heading: New qualified fuel cell motor vehicle credit
children.label: b New qualified fuel cell motor vehicle credit
A
children.text: $8,000 ($4,000 in the case of a vehicle placed in service after ), if such vehicle has a gross vehicle weight rating of not more than 8,500 pounds,December 31, 2009

children.label: B
children.text: $10,000, if such vehicle has a gross vehicle weight rating of more than 8,500 pounds but not more than 14,000 pounds,

children.label: C
children.text: $20,000, if such vehicle has a gross vehicle weight rating of more than 14,000 pounds but not more than 26,000 pounds, and

children.label: D
children.text: $40,000, if such vehicle has a gross vehicle weight rating of more than 26,000 pounds.

children.label: i
children.text: $1,000, if such vehicle achieves at least 150 percent but less than 175 percent of the 2002 model year city fuel economy,

children.label: ii
children.text: $1,500, if such vehicle achieves at least 175 percent but less than 200 percent of the 2002 model year city fuel economy,

children.label: iii
children.text: $2,000, if such vehicle achieves at least 200 percent but less than 225 percent of the 2002 model year city fuel economy,

children.label: iv
children.text: $2,500, if such vehicle achieves at least 225 percent but less than 250 percent of the 2002 model year city fuel economy,

children.label: v
children.text: $3,000, if such vehicle achieves at least 250 percent but less than 275 percent of the 2002 model year city fuel economy,

children.label: vi
children.text: $3,500, if such vehicle achieves at least 275 percent but less than 300 percent of the 2002 model year city fuel economy, and

children.label: vii
children.text: $4,000, if such vehicle achieves at least 300 percent of the 2002 model year city fuel economy.

children.heading: 2002 model year city fuel economy
children.label: B 2002 model year city fuel economy
i
children.text: In the case of a passenger automobile:If vehicle inertia weight class is:The 2002 model year city fuel economy is:1,500 or 1,750 lbs45.2 mpg2,000 lbs39.6 mpg2,250 lbs35.2 mpg2,500 lbs31.7 mpg2,750 lbs28.8 mpg3,000 lbs26.4 mpg3,500 lbs22.6 mpg4,000 lbs19.8 mpg4,500 lbs17.6 mpg5,000 lbs15.9 mpg5,500 lbs14.4 mpg6,000 lbs13.2 mpg6,500 lbs12.2 mpg7,000 to 8,500 lbs11.3 mpg.

children.label: ii
children.text: In the case of a light truck:If vehicle inertia weight class is:The 2002 model year city fuel economy is:1,500 or 1,750 lbs39.4 mpg2,000 lbs35.2 mpg2,250 lbs31.8 mpg2,500 lbs29.0 mpg2,750 lbs26.8 mpg3,000 lbs24.9 mpg3,500 lbs21.8 mpg4,000 lbs19.4 mpg4,500 lbs17.6 mpg5,000 lbs16.1 mpg5,500 lbs14.8 mpg6,000 lbs13.7 mpg6,500 lbs12.8 mpg7,000 to 8,500 lbs12.1 mpg.

children.heading: Vehicle inertia weight class
children.label: C Vehicle inertia weight class
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (B), the term “vehicle inertia weight class” has the same meaning as when defined in regulations prescribed by the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency for purposes of the administration of title II of the Clean Air Act ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 7521

children.heading: New qualified fuel cell motor vehicle
children.label: 3 New qualified fuel cell motor vehicle
A
children.text: which is propelled by power derived from 1 or more cells which convert chemical energy directly into electricity by combining oxygen with hydrogen fuel which is stored on board the vehicle in any form and may or may not require reformation prior to use,

children.label: B
children.text: which, in the case of a passenger automobile or light truck, has received on or after the date of the enactment of this section a certificate that such vehicle meets or exceeds the Bin 5 Tier II emission level established in regulations prescribed by the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency under section 202(i) of the Clean Air Act for that make and model year vehicle,

children.label: C
children.text: the original use of which commences with the taxpayer,

children.label: D
children.text: which is acquired for use or lease by the taxpayer and not for resale, and

children.label: E
children.text: which is made by a manufacturer.

children.heading: New advanced lean burn technology motor vehicle credit
children.label: c New advanced lean burn technology motor vehicle credit

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of subsection (a), the new advanced lean burn technology motor vehicle credit determined under this subsection for the taxable year is the credit amount determined under paragraph (2) with respect to a new advanced lean burn technology motor vehicle placed in service by the taxpayer during the taxable year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The credit amount determined under this paragraph shall be determined in accordance with the following table:In the case of a vehicle which achievesa fuel economy (expressed as apercentage of the 2002 model yearcity fuel economy) of—The credit amount is—At least 125 percent but less than 150 percent$400At least 150 percent but less than 175 percent$800At least 175 percent but less than 200 percent$1,200At least 200 percent but less than 225 percent$1,600At least 225 percent but less than 250 percent$2,000At least 250 percent$2,400.

children.heading: 2002 model year city fuel economy
children.label: ii 2002 model year city fuel economy
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), the 2002 model year city fuel economy with respect to a vehicle shall be determined on a gasoline gallon equivalent basis as determined by the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency using the tables provided in subsection (b)(2)(B) with respect to such vehicle.

children.heading: Conservation credit
children.label: B Conservation credit
children.text: The amount determined under subparagraph (A) with respect to a new advanced lean burn technology motor vehicle shall be increased by the conservation credit amount determined in accordance with the following table:In the case of a vehicle which achievesa lifetime fuel savings (expressed ingallons of gasoline) of—The conservation credit amount is—At least 1,200 but less than 1,800$250At least 1,800 but less than 2,400$500At least 2,400 but less than 3,000$750At least 3,000$1,000.

children.heading: New advanced lean burn technology motor vehicle
children.label: 3 New advanced lean burn technology motor vehicle
i
children.text: is designed to operate primarily using more air than is necessary for complete combustion of the fuel,

children.label: ii
children.text: incorporates direct injection,

children.label: iii
children.text: achieves at least 125 percent of the 2002 model year city fuel economy,

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of a vehicle having a gross vehicle weight rating of 6,000 pounds or less, the Bin 5 Tier II emission standard established in regulations prescribed by the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency under section 202(i) of the Clean Air Act for that make and model year vehicle, and

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a vehicle having a gross vehicle weight rating of more than 6,000 pounds but not more than 8,500 pounds, the Bin 8 Tier II emission standard which is so established,

children.label: B
children.text: the original use of which commences with the taxpayer,

children.label: C
children.text: which is acquired for use or lease by the taxpayer and not for resale, and

children.label: D
children.text: which is made by a manufacturer.

children.label: A
children.text: 120,000 divided by the 2002 model year city fuel economy for the vehicle inertia weight class, over

children.label: B
children.text: 120,000 divided by the city fuel economy for such vehicle.

children.heading: New qualified hybrid motor vehicle credit
children.label: d New qualified hybrid motor vehicle credit

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of subsection (a), the new qualified hybrid motor vehicle credit determined under this subsection for the taxable year is the credit amount determined under paragraph (2) with respect to a new qualified hybrid motor vehicle placed in service by the taxpayer during the taxable year.

children.heading: Credit amount for passenger automobiles and light trucks
children.label: A Credit amount for passenger automobiles and light trucks

children.heading: Fuel economy
children.label: i Fuel economy
children.text: The amount determined under this clause is the amount which would be determined under subsection (c)(2)(A) if such vehicle were a vehicle referred to in such subsection.

children.heading: Conservation credit
children.label: ii Conservation credit
children.text: The amount determined under this clause is the amount which would be determined under subsection (c)(2)(B) if such vehicle were a vehicle referred to in such subsection.

children.heading: Credit amount for other motor vehicles
children.label: B Credit amount for other motor vehicles

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In the case of any new qualified hybrid motor vehicle to which subparagraph (A) does not apply, the amount determined under this paragraph is the amount equal to the applicable percentage of the qualified incremental hybrid cost of the vehicle as certified under clause (v).

children.label: I
children.text: 20 percent if the vehicle achieves an increase in city fuel economy relative to a comparable vehicle of at least 30 percent but less than 40 percent,

children.label: II
children.text: 30 percent if the vehicle achieves such an increase of at least 40 percent but less than 50 percent, and

children.label: III
children.text: 40 percent if the vehicle achieves such an increase of at least 50 percent.

children.label: I
children.text: $7,500, if such vehicle has a gross vehicle weight rating of not more than 14,000 pounds,

children.label: II
children.text: $15,000, if such vehicle has a gross vehicle weight rating of more than 14,000 pounds but not more than 26,000 pounds, and

children.label: III
children.text: $30,000, if such vehicle has a gross vehicle weight rating of more than 26,000 pounds.

children.heading: Comparable vehicle
children.label: iv Comparable vehicle
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “comparable vehicle” means, with respect to any new qualified hybrid motor vehicle, any vehicle which is powered solely by a gasoline or diesel internal combustion engine and which is comparable in weight, size, and use to such vehicle.

children.heading: Certification
children.label: v Certification
children.text: A certification described in clause (i) shall be made by the manufacturer and shall be determined in accordance with guidance prescribed by the Secretary. Such guidance shall specify procedures and methods for calculating fuel economy savings and incremental hybrid costs.

children.label: I
children.text: an internal combustion or heat engine using consumable fuel, and

children.label: II
children.text: a rechargeable energy storage system,

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of a vehicle having a gross vehicle weight rating of 6,000 pounds or less, the Bin 5 Tier II emission standard established in regulations prescribed by the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency under section 202(i) of the Clean Air Act for that make and model year vehicle, and

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a vehicle having a gross vehicle weight rating of more than 6,000 pounds but not more than 8,500 pounds, the Bin 8 Tier II emission standard which is so established,

children.label: I
children.text: 4 percent in the case of a vehicle to which paragraph (2)(A) applies,

children.label: II
children.text: 10 percent in the case of a vehicle which has a gross vehicle weight rating of more than 8,500 pounds and not more than 14,000 pounds, and

children.label: III
children.text: 15 percent in the case of a vehicle in excess of 14,000 pounds,

children.label: iv
children.text: which, in the case of a vehicle to which paragraph (2)(B) applies, has an internal combustion or heat engine which has received a certificate of conformity under the Clean Air Act as meeting the emission standards set in the regulations prescribed by the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency for 2004 through 2007 model year diesel heavy duty engines or ottocycle heavy duty engines, as applicable,

children.label: v
children.text: the original use of which commences with the taxpayer,

children.label: vi
children.text: which is acquired for use or lease by the taxpayer and not for resale, and

children.label: vii
children.text: which is made by a manufacturer.

children.heading: Consumable fuel
children.label: B Consumable fuel
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A)(i)(I), the term “consumable fuel” means any solid, liquid, or gaseous matter which releases energy when consumed by an auxiliary power unit.

children.heading: Certain passenger automobiles and light trucks
children.label: i Certain passenger automobiles and light trucks
children.text: In the case of a vehicle to which paragraph (2)(A) applies, the term “maximum available power” means the maximum power available from the rechargeable energy storage system, during a standard 10 second pulse power or equivalent test, divided by such maximum power and the SAE net power of the heat engine.

children.heading: Other motor vehicles
children.label: ii Other motor vehicles
children.text: In the case of a vehicle to which paragraph (2)(B) applies, the term “maximum available power” means the maximum power available from the rechargeable energy storage system, during a standard 10 second pulse power or equivalent test, divided by the vehicle’s total traction power. For purposes of the preceding sentence, the term “total traction power” means the sum of the peak power from the rechargeable energy storage system and the heat engine peak power of the vehicle, except that if such storage system is the sole means by which the vehicle can be driven, the total traction power is the peak power of such storage system.

children.heading: Exclusion of plug-in vehicles
children.label: D Exclusion of plug-in vehicles
children.text: Any vehicle with respect to which a credit is allowable under section 30D (determined without regard to subsection (c) thereof) shall not be taken into account under this section.

children.heading: New qualified alternative fuel motor vehicle credit
children.label: e New qualified alternative fuel motor vehicle credit

children.heading: Allowance of credit
children.label: 1 Allowance of credit
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (5), the new qualified alternative fuel motor vehicle credit determined under this subsection is an amount equal to the applicable percentage of the incremental cost of any new qualified alternative fuel motor vehicle placed in service by the taxpayer during the taxable year.

children.label: A
children.text: 50 percent, plus

children.label: i
children.text: has received a certificate of conformity under the Clean Air Act and meets or exceeds the most stringent standard available for certification under the Clean Air Act for that make and model year vehicle (other than a zero emission standard), or

children.label: ii
children.text: has received an order certifying the vehicle as meeting the same requirements as vehicles which may be sold or leased in California and meets or exceeds the most stringent standard available for certification under the State laws of California (enacted in accordance with a waiver granted under section 209(b) of the Clean Air Act) for that make and model year vehicle (other than a zero emission standard).

children.label: A
children.text: $5,000, if such vehicle has a gross vehicle weight rating of not more than 8,500 pounds,

children.label: B
children.text: $10,000, if such vehicle has a gross vehicle weight rating of more than 8,500 pounds but not more than 14,000 pounds,

children.label: C
children.text: $25,000, if such vehicle has a gross vehicle weight rating of more than 14,000 pounds but not more than 26,000 pounds, and

children.label: D
children.text: $40,000, if such vehicle has a gross vehicle weight rating of more than 26,000 pounds.

children.heading: New qualified alternative fuel motor vehicle
children.label: 4 New qualified alternative fuel motor vehicle
i
children.text: which is only capable of operating on an alternative fuel,

children.label: ii
children.text: the original use of which commences with the taxpayer,

children.label: iii
children.text: which is acquired by the taxpayer for use or lease, but not for resale, and

children.label: iv
children.text: which is made by a manufacturer.

children.heading: Alternative fuel
children.label: B Alternative fuel
children.text: The term “alternative fuel” means compressed natural gas, liquefied natural gas, liquefied petroleum gas, hydrogen, and any liquid at least 85 percent of the volume of which consists of methanol.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a 75/25 mixed-fuel vehicle, 70 percent of the credit which would have been allowed under this subsection if such vehicle was a qualified alternative fuel motor vehicle, and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a 90/10 mixed-fuel vehicle, 90 percent of the credit which would have been allowed under this subsection if such vehicle was a qualified alternative fuel motor vehicle.

children.label: i
children.text: is certified by the manufacturer as being able to perform efficiently in normal operation on a combination of an alternative fuel and a petroleum-based fuel,

children.label: I
children.text: has received a certificate of conformity under the Clean Air Act, or

children.label: II
children.text: has received an order certifying the vehicle as meeting the same requirements as vehicles which may be sold or leased in California and meets or exceeds the low emission vehicle standard under section 88.105–94 of title 40, Code of Federal Regulations, for that make and model year vehicle,

children.label: iii
children.text: the original use of which commences with the taxpayer,

children.label: iv
children.text: which is acquired by the taxpayer for use or lease, but not for resale, and

children.label: v
children.text: which is made by a manufacturer.

children.heading: 75/25 mixed-fuel vehicle
children.label: C 75/25 mixed-fuel vehicle
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “75/25 mixed-fuel vehicle” means a mixed-fuel vehicle which operates using at least 75 percent alternative fuel and not more than 25 percent petroleum-based fuel.

children.heading: 90/10 mixed-fuel vehicle
children.label: D 90/10 mixed-fuel vehicle
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “90/10 mixed-fuel vehicle” means a mixed-fuel vehicle which operates using at least 90 percent alternative fuel and not more than 10 percent petroleum-based fuel.

children.heading: Limitation on number of new qualified hybrid and advanced lean-burn technology vehicles eligible for credit
children.label: f Limitation on number of new qualified hybrid and advanced lean-burn technology vehicles eligible for credit

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the case of a qualified vehicle sold during the phaseout period, only the applicable percentage of the credit otherwise allowable under subsection (c) or (d) shall be allowed.

children.heading: Phaseout period
children.label: 2 Phaseout period
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the phaseout period is the period beginning with the second calendar quarter following the calendar quarter which includes the first date on which the number of qualified vehicles manufactured by the manufacturer of the vehicle referred to in paragraph (1) sold for use in the United States after , is at least 60,000.December 31, 2005

children.label: A
children.text: 50 percent for the first 2 calendar quarters of the phaseout period,

children.label: B
children.text: 25 percent for the 3d and 4th calendar quarters of the phaseout period, and

children.label: C
children.text: 0 percent for each calendar quarter thereafter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, all persons treated as a single employer under subsection (a) or (b) of section 52 or subsection (m) or () of section 414 shall be treated as a single manufacturer.o

children.heading: Inclusion of foreign corporations
children.label: B Inclusion of foreign corporations
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), in applying subsections (a) and (b) of section 52 to this section, section 1563 shall be applied without regard to subsection (b)(2)(C) thereof.

children.heading: Qualified vehicle
children.label: 5 Qualified vehicle
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “qualified vehicle” means any new qualified hybrid motor vehicle (described in subsection (d)(2)(A)) and any new advanced lean burn technology motor vehicle.

children.heading: Business credit treated as part of general business credit
children.label: 1 Business credit treated as part of general business credit
children.text: So much of the credit which would be allowed under subsection (a) for any taxable year (determined without regard to this subsection) that is attributable to property of a character subject to an allowance for depreciation shall be treated as a credit listed in section 38(b) for such taxable year (and not allowed under subsection (a)).

children.heading: Personal credit
children.label: 2 Personal credit
children.text: For purposes of this title, the credit allowed under subsection (a) for any taxable year (determined after application of paragraph (1)) shall be treated as a credit allowable under subpart A for such taxable year.

children.heading: Other definitions and special rules
children.label: h Other definitions and special rules

children.heading: Motor vehicle
children.label: 1 Motor vehicle
children.text: The term “motor vehicle” means any vehicle which is manufactured primarily for use on public streets, roads, and highways (not including a vehicle operated exclusively on a rail or rails) and which has at least 4 wheels.

children.heading: City fuel economy
children.label: 2 City fuel economy
children.text: The city fuel economy with respect to any vehicle shall be measured in a manner which is substantially similar to the manner city fuel economy is measured in accordance with procedures under part 600 of subchapter Q of chapter I of title 40, Code of Federal Regulations, as in effect on the date of the enactment of this section.

children.heading: Other terms
children.label: 3 Other terms
children.text: The terms “automobile”, “passenger automobile”, “medium duty passenger vehicle”, “light truck”, and “manufacturer” have the meanings given such terms in regulations prescribed by the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency for purposes of the administration of title II of the Clean Air Act ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 7521

children.heading: Reduction in basis
children.label: 4 Reduction in basis
children.text: For purposes of this subtitle, the basis of any property for which a credit is allowable under subsection (a) shall be reduced by the amount of such credit so allowed (determined without regard to subsection (g)).

children.label: A
children.text: for any incremental cost taken into account in computing the amount of the credit determined under subsection (e) shall be reduced by the amount of such credit attributable to such cost, and

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to a vehicle described under subsection (b) or (c), shall be reduced by the amount of credit allowed under subsection (a) for such vehicle for the taxable year (determined without regard to subsection (g)).

children.heading: Property used by tax-exempt entity
children.label: 6 Property used by tax-exempt entity
children.text: In the case of a vehicle whose use is described in paragraph (3) or (4) of section 50(b) and which is not subject to a lease, the person who sold such vehicle to the person or entity using such vehicle shall be treated as the taxpayer that placed such vehicle in service, but only if such person clearly discloses to such person or entity in a document the amount of any credit allowable under subsection (a) with respect to such vehicle (determined without regard to subsection (g)). For purposes of subsection (g), property to which this paragraph applies shall be treated as of a character subject to an allowance for depreciation.

children.heading: Property used outside United States, etc., not qualified
children.label: 7 Property used outside United States, etc., not qualified
children.text: No credit shall be allowable under subsection (a) with respect to any property referred to in section 50(b)(1) or with respect to the portion of the cost of any property taken into account under section 179.

children.heading: Recapture
children.label: 8 Recapture
children.text: The Secretary shall, by regulations, provide for recapturing the benefit of any credit allowable under subsection (a) with respect to any property which ceases to be property eligible for such credit (including recapture in the case of a lease period of less than the economic life of a vehicle).

children.heading: Election to not take credit
children.label: 9 Election to not take credit
children.text: No credit shall be allowed under subsection (a) for any vehicle if the taxpayer elects to not have this section apply to such vehicle.

children.heading: Interaction with air quality and motor vehicle safety standards
children.label: 10 Interaction with air quality and motor vehicle safety standards
A
children.text: the applicable provisions of the Clean Air Act for the applicable make and model year of the vehicle (or applicable air quality provisions of State law in the case of a State which has adopted such provision under a waiver under section 209(b) of the Clean Air Act), and

children.label: B
children.text: the motor vehicle safety provisions of sections 30101 through 30169 of title 49, United States Code.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), the Secretary shall promulgate such regulations as necessary to carry out the provisions of this section.

children.heading: Coordination in prescription of certain regulations
children.label: 2 Coordination in prescription of certain regulations
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury, in coordination with the Secretary of Transportation and the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency, shall prescribe such regulations as necessary to determine whether a motor vehicle meets the requirements to be eligible for a credit under this section.
in the case of a new qualified fuel cell motor vehicle (as described in subsection (b)), ,December 31, 2021
in the case of a new advanced lean burn technology motor vehicle (as described in subsection (c)) or a new qualified hybrid motor vehicle (as described in subsection (d)(2)(A)), ,December 31, 2010
in the case of a new qualified hybrid motor vehicle (as described in subsection (d)(2)(B)), , andDecember 31, 2009
in the case of a new qualified alternative fuel vehicle (as described in subsection (e)), .December 31, 2010
